description: Mechanical conflict-detector table (ct-intention-extraction.md §6.1 stage 1). Curated, versioned, per-check DATA — the sibling of src/ctkr/normalization.json (open decision e, recommended: sibling data file). Each entry is a cheap, deterministic, HIGH-PRECISION claim-vs-structure check: a strong intention signal (a name, a docstring claim, a decorator) contradicting a tier-I structural fact (crossing edges, field-flow direction, caller counts). Emits conflict-candidate rows for T5b's LM adjudication (§6.1 stage 2) to confirm — the table proposes, the strong model disposes. `check` selects the engine routine; the remaining fields are its parameters. Adding a check kind is code; adding a pattern/threshold to an existing kind is a table edit.
severity_note: port-critical = a name/doc claims behavior the structure refutes and the element crosses a boundary (external code observes the truth); advisory = softer disagreement (stale docs, deprecated-but-used). port-critical conflicts force the element out of 'structure-clear' (§5.2 override) and head the brief's Warnings section (§6.2).

detectors.message_template: named/typed as a read ({read_tokens}) but structurally writes external state via {n} {kinds} edge(s) across the subsystem boundary. Trust structure for what happens, the name for what was meant: the port must reproduce the write (tier-I, externally observed) and may rename honestly.
detectors.rationale: A 'Cache'/'get*'/'read*' that mutates external state is re-implemented wrongly by any builder who trusts the name (§6.1 canonical example).

detectors.message_template: docstring claims purity/statelessness ({claim}) but structurally writes state via {n} {kinds} edge(s). The port must reproduce the write; the 'pure' claim is stale or aspirational.
detectors.rationale: A 'pure'/'stateless' docstring vs. observed state edges is a §6.1 mechanical check.

detectors.message_template: marked @deprecated but still has {n} caller(s) via {kinds}. The port must decide: reproduce, or migrate callers off it — do not silently drop.
detectors.rationale: @deprecated on exports with live callers is a §6.1 declared-debt conflict; advisory because it is a known-state divergence, not a lie about behavior.

detectors.message_template: docstring documents {doc_arity} parameter(s) but the signature declares {sig_arity}. The docs have drifted from the contract; trust the signature for shape.
detectors.rationale: Docstring parameter list vs. actual signature arity is a §6.1 detectable drift (S4 rots when signatures move).
